posts.title: ARD Strike Called Off Following Favorable Resolutions
posts.body:
The Association of Resident Doctors, OAUTHC, has officially suspended its industrial action that began earlier this month.

Following a protracted series of negotiations with the management and the federal government representatives, significant headway has been made regarding the payment of outstanding allowances and the implementation of the new hazard allowance scale.

Members are expected to resume their clinical duties starting at 8:00 AM tomorrow. The executive committee extends profound gratitude to all members for their unwavering solidarity and commitment to standardizing healthcare practice in the institution.

posts.title: Why Continuous Medical Education Remains Our Priority
posts.body:
In modern medicine, resting on one's laurels is tantamount to obsolescence. The rapid pace of pharmacological discoveries and surgical innovations necessitates a culture of continuous learning.

The ARD has partnered with foreign health trusts to grant our doctors unfettered access to world-class journals and weekly virtual roundtables. This commitment shapes our approach to patient care, ensuring that OAUTHC remains a vanguard of medical excellence in Nigeria.
